text: this is a simple video of a fats right lower lobe wedge excision this lady had a metastasis in the lower lobe and we were just going to wedge it out without an anatomical resection however we released the inferior pulmonary ligament

as you can see here to get extra space for the wedge excision this is us palpating through our usual Vaxport incisions and so that we know exactly where the mass is we've just made some diathermy marks on the lung just so that our staple lines get above the metastasis leaving a good margin without requiring an anatomical resection we actually use our standard

that's the back to me incisions an anterior port as you can see in front there this is a water suture try stapler with the medium thick 60 millimeter staples and you can see by the diathermy marks that we are well away from the tumor and we're doing the exact desired excision margin you can see two four o'clock the diaphragm and the chest wall
There would be no survival advantage in doing an anatomical resection.
The only indication would be if it was not possible to do a wedge.
This particular mass was palpable, but if they're impalpable we can place coils, wires, methylene blue, radioactive markers to make sure that we can localise it without requiring a thoracotomy.
And this particular patient did extremely well post-operatively going home on day three.
And this is us removing the tumour.
